task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: Random is an SP method whose Count parameter specifies the size in bytes of the random byte sequence. Count is a uinteger, and Opal requires support for Count values up to 32. Without BufferOut, the successful Result is the generated byte sequence of the requested size. If BufferOut is specified, Result SHALL be empty. Attempts to use unsupported Random parameters fail with INVALID_PARAMETER.

source_snippets.text: 5.6.4.1 Random Number Related Method Group - Random (SP Method) This section describes the method used to generate random numbers. This method returns a sequence of random bytes of a specified size. The quality of random numbers generated is under the purview of the conformance profile. ThisSP.Random[ Count : uinteger, BufferOut = cell_block ] => [ Result : bytes ]
5.6.4.1.1 Count This parameter specifies the size, bytes, of the sequence of random bytes to be generated.

5.6.4.1.2 BufferOut This value identifies a specific cell or range of cells to which the generated bytes are stored. All cells identified by the BufferOut parameter and to be written with the method result SHALL be of type bytes. Cells are filled from the lowest column number to the highest column number.
5.6.4.1.3.1 Result This is the value randomly generated by invocation of this method. If the BufferOut parameter is specified, Result SHALL be empty.

5.1.3.82 uinteger This is the base type that is used to represent an unsigned integer. Table 146 uinteger | UID | Name | Format | |------------------------------|-----------|------------| | 00 00 00 05 00 00 00 05 | uinteger | Base_Type |

4.2.9.1 Random The TPer SHALL implement the Random method with the constraints stated in this subsection. TPer support of the following parameters is Mandatory: • Count Attempts to use unsupported parameters SHALL result in a method failure response with TCG status INVALID_PARAMETER. The TPer SHALL support Count parameter values less than or equal to 32.
4.3.4.1 Random Refer to section 4.2.9.1 for additional constraints imposed on the Random method.

output_schema.label: PASS or FAIL
output_schema.rationale: short explanation grounded in source text
output_schema.concerns: empty string if none; otherwise state ambiguity
